Variable Inclusion: §2.2 Requirements vs. Ellis Implementation

Data Primer — Part 2 of 2

Published

July 18, 2026

This document is the traceability record linking the variable requirements specified in §2.2 of the statistical analysis instructions to their concrete implementation in the Ellis lane (manipulation/2-ellis.R). One table is provided for each variable category in §2.2. The companion document with actual distributions is: Univariate Distributions.

Sample Construction Flowchart (§3.1)

The following CONSORT-style diagram documents the sequential exclusion criteria applied in 2-ellis.R to produce the analytical sample. Each step records the number of respondents remaining and excluded.

Step Criterion n remaining n excluded
0 Full pooled sample (CCHS 2010 + 2014) 126,431 —
1 Age filter: DHHGAGE codes 2–15 (ages 15–75) ~122,500 ~3,900
2 Employment filter: LOP_015 = 1 (worked past 3 months) ~64,248 ~58,200
3 Proxy filter: ADM_PRX != 1 (not proxy interview) ~64,248 0
4 Outcome completeness: all 8 LOP day-count variables non-missing (code != 99) 63,843 405

Note: Exact counts at steps 1–3 vary slightly by run depending on NA handling. The final analytic n = 63,843 is stable. Zero proxy respondents were found in the employed subsample. The 405-row exclusion at step 4 reflects genuine non-response (code 99 = “NOT STATED”) on at least one LOP day-count variable — not structural survey-skip NAs, which are coded as zero.

Column definitions:

  • Requested — Variable as described in §2.2 (conceptual name or derived label).
  • 2011 — Actual column name in the CCHS 2010–2011 PUMF after janitor::clean_names().
  • 2014 — Actual column name in the CCHS 2013–2014 PUMF after janitor::clean_names().
  • In Study — Column name in the analytical dataset (cchs_analytic). Continuous raw variables retain their PUMF name; factor-recoded variables receive a new semantic name.
  • Note — Implementation status and any discrepancy between white-list and actual PUMF name.

Status symbols used in the Note column:

  • ✅ Delivered as-is — white-list name matches PUMF exactly; variable present and populated in both cycles.
  • ⚠️ Alias required — PUMF uses a different name than the white-list entry; alias map or whitelist fix needed.
  • ❌ Absent from PUMF — variable suppressed in the Public Use Microdata File; not obtainable without RDC access.

1. Outcome — Total Days Absent (All Health Reasons)

Days absent from work (past 3 months) – all health reasons combined (count) | LOP module – sum across all reason categories

The primary outcome days_absent_total is constructed by Ellis as the row-wise sum of eight Loss of Productivity (LOP) component variables. When all eight are NA for a respondent, the outcome is set to 0 (structural zero — no reported absence). Values outside the 0–90 day range are set to NA with a warning. Both PUMF cycles use identical variable names for all eight components.

Requested 2011 2014 In Study Note
Days absent — chronic condition (component) lopg040 lopg040 lopg040 ✅ Also the sensitivity outcome (§2 row 2)
Days absent — injury (component) lopg070 lopg070 lopg070 ✅
Days absent — cold (component) lopg082 lopg082 lopg082 ✅
Days absent — flu / influenza (component) lopg083 lopg083 lopg083 ✅
Days absent — stomach flu / gastroenteritis (component) lopg084 lopg084 lopg084 ✅
Days absent — respiratory infection (component) lopg085 lopg085 lopg085 ✅
Days absent — other infectious disease (component) lopg086 lopg086 lopg086 ✅
Days absent — other physical / mental health reason (component) lopg100 lopg100 lopg100 ✅
Derived outcome — — days_absent_total ✅ Ellis-computed row-wise sum of the 8 LOP components above

2. Outcome (Sensitivity) — Days Absent Due to Chronic Conditions Only

Days absent from work due to chronic conditions only | LOP module – chronic condition reason only

The sensitivity outcome days_absent_chronic is taken directly from the single LOP component that records days lost specifically because of the respondent’s own chronic condition.

Requested 2011 2014 In Study Note
Days absent — chronic condition reason only lopg040 lopg040 days_absent_chronic ✅ Single-component copy of lopg040; stored separately for sensitivity analysis

3. Primary Exposure — 19 Chronic Conditions

19 chronic conditions (binary each): arthritis, asthma, cardiovascular disease/stroke, hypertension, fibromyalgia, diabetes, migraine, back problems, cancer, COPD, mental illness (depression/bipolar/etc.), bowel disorders, digestive diseases, chronic fatigue syndrome, multiple chemical sensitivities, mood disorder, anxiety disorder, intestinal/stomach ulcer [check exact groupings in Appendix 3 of the thesis] | CCC module

All CCC variables are recoded to logical (TRUE / FALSE); numeric response codes 6, 7, 8, 9, and 96–99 are set to NA. Two conditions — ccc_300 (other mental illness) and ccc_185 (digestive disease) — are suppressed in the PUMF and cannot be recovered without RDC access. Cardiovascular disease and stroke are represented as two separate CCC variables in the PUMF.

Requested condition 2011 2014 In Study Note
Asthma ccc_031 ccc_031 cc_asthma ✅ logical: TRUE / FALSE
Fibromyalgia ccc_041 ccc_041 cc_fibromyalgia ✅ logical: TRUE / FALSE
Arthritis (excl. fibromyalgia) ccc_051 ccc_051 cc_arthritis ✅ logical: TRUE / FALSE
Back problems (excl. fibromyalgia/arthritis) ccc_061 ccc_061 cc_back_problems ✅
Hypertension ccc_071 ccc_071 cc_hypertension ✅
Migraine ccc_081 ccc_081 cc_migraine ✅
COPD / chronic bronchitis / emphysema ccc_091 ccc_091 cc_copd ✅
Diabetes ccc_101 ccc_101 cc_diabetes ✅
Cardiovascular disease / heart disease ccc_121 ccc_121 cc_heart_disease ✅
Cancer (any type) ccc_131 ccc_131 cc_cancer ✅
Intestinal / stomach ulcer ccc_141 ccc_141 cc_ulcer ✅
Stroke (cardiovascular) ccc_151 ccc_151 cc_stroke ✅
Bowel disorder (Crohn’s / colitis / IBS) ccc_171 ccc_171 cc_bowel_disorder ✅
Chronic fatigue syndrome (CFS) ccc_251 ccc_251 cc_fatigue_syndrome ✅
Multiple chemical sensitivities (MCS) ccc_261 ccc_261 cc_chem_sensitivity ✅
Mood disorder (depression / bipolar / mania / dysthymia) ccc_280 ccc_280 cc_mood_disorder ✅
Anxiety disorder (phobia / OCD / panic disorder) ccc_290 ccc_290 cc_anxiety ✅
Other mental illness (schizophrenia, etc.) ❌ absent ❌ absent ❌ absent ❌ ccc_300 suppressed in both PUMF cycles; requires RDC access
Digestive disease (other than ulcer / bowel disorder) ❌ absent ❌ absent ❌ absent ❌ ccc_185 suppressed in both PUMF cycles; requires RDC access

Coverage summary: 17 of 19 conditions are available in the PUMF. Two conditions (ccc_300, ccc_185) require RDC access. All 17 present columns use the cc_* prefix and are stored as R logical (not factor); prevalence calculations must compare x == TRUE / x == FALSE.

4. Predisposing Variables

Age (continuous, recoded in 3 categories: 15–24, 25–54, 55–75); sex; marital status; household size; number of children (< 5 yrs, 6–11 yrs, ≥ 12 yrs); education; immigration status; ethnic origin (White vs. visible minority); homeownership; student status | Main questionnaire

Age is available in PUMF only as a grouped derived variable (dhhgage); single-year resolution requires restricted RDC access. dhhdfc12p (children ≥ 12 yrs) has no PUMF equivalent. Student status (sdcdgstud) is suppressed in the PUMF and renders all-NA.

Requested 2011 2014 In Study Note
Age (grouped; recoded to 15–24, 25–54, 55–75) dhhgage dhhgage age_group_3 ✅ Ordered factor: “15-24” / “25-54” / “55-75”; raw dhhgage also retained as integer code
Sex dhh_sex dhh_sex sex ✅ Factor: Male / Female
Marital status dhhgms dhhgms marital_status ✅ Factor: Single / Married / Common-law / Widowed-Divorced-Separated
Household size (number of persons) dhhghsz dhhghsz household_size ✅ Ordered factor: 1 / 2 / 3 / 4 / 5+ (recoded from dhhghsz; groups 5–8 collapsed to “5+”)
Children ≤ 5 yrs in household dhhgle5 dhhgle5 dhhgle5 ✅ Numeric (0 = none, 1 = one or more); present and populated in both cycles
Children 6–11 yrs in household dhhg611 dhhg611 dhhg611 ✅ Numeric (0 = none, 1 = one or more); present and populated in both cycles
Children ≥ 12 yrs in household (count) unknown unknown ❌ absent ❌ White-list used dhhdfc12p; no PUMF equivalent found in either cycle
Education level (4 categories) edudh04 edudh04 education ✅ Ordered factor: Less than secondary → Post-secondary graduate. ⚠️ Cross-cycle label discrepancy for code 3 (“Other post-secondary” in 2010 vs “Some post-secondary” in 2014)
Immigration status sdcfimm sdcfimm immigration_status ✅ Factor: Non-immigrant / Immigrant / Non-permanent resident
Ethnic origin (White vs. visible minority) sdcgcgt sdcgcgt visible_minority ✅ Alias sdcdgcb ← sdcgcgt resolves; Factor: White / Visible minority
Living arrangements dhhglvg dhhglvg living_arrangements ✅ Factor (8 levels): Unattached alone / Unattached with others / Couple no children / Couple with children / Single parent / Child in parent-sibling household / Child in two-parent household / Other. Note: The Project Proposal (§2.2) specifies “homeownership” as a predisposing variable; however, no house-ownership construct exists in the CCHS PUMF. living_arrangements (household composition) is included as the closest available proxy, per agreement with M.-A. Blanchette (2026-05-15 review).
Student status ❌ absent ❌ absent student_status (all NA) ❌ sdcdgstud not present in PUMF; column all-NA in current dataset

5. Facilitating Variables

Household income (5 categories); province/territory of residence (11 categories); having a family doctor; employment type (self-employed vs. employee); work schedule (full-time vs. part-time); occupation category; job stress level; fruit/vegetable consumption; alcohol use; smoking status; BMI/obesity category; physical activity level | Main questionnaire

All 12 facilitating variables are populated in the current pipeline output. The job stress variable used is GEN_09 (perceived work stress), stored as work_stress, not GEN_07 (life stress). Province is retained as a 13-level factor (includes the combined Yukon/NWT/Nunavut territory grouping). Employment type has 2 levels in the PUMF (Employee / Self-employed); “Unpaid family worker” is not present.

Requested 2011 2014 In Study Note
Household income — derived 5-category incghh incghh income_hh ✅ Alias incdghh ← incghh resolves; Ordered factor: < $20k → $80k+
Province / territory of residence geogprv geogprv province ✅ Alias geodgprv ← geogprv resolves; 13-level factor (includes NWT/Yukon/Nunavut combined)
Having a regular family doctor hcu_1aa hcu_1aa has_family_doctor ✅ Factor: Yes / No
Employment type (self-employed vs. employee) lbsg31 lbsg31 employment_type ✅ Factor: Employee / Self-employed (2 levels in PUMF; “Unpaid family worker” suppressed)
Work schedule (full-time vs. part-time) lbsdpft lbsdpft work_schedule ✅ Alias lbfdgft ← lbsdpft resolves; Factor: Full-time / Part-time
Occupation category (5-group SOC) lbsgsoc lbsgsoc occupation_category ✅ Factor: Mgmt/Educ/Arts / Business/Finance / Sales/Services / Trades/Transport / Primary/Processing (2010 descriptive labels applied to both cycles; codes identical)
Job / work stress level gen_09 gen_09 work_stress ✅ GEN_09 = perceived work stress; ordered factor: Not at all / Not very / A bit / Quite a bit / Extremely stressful
Total daily fruit & vegetable consumption fvcgtot fvcgtot fruit_veg_daily ✅ Alias fvcdgtot ← fvcgtot resolves; 3-category derived ordinal (< 5 / 5–10 / > 10 servings per day)
Alcohol use (type of drinker — derived) alcdttm alcdttm alcohol_type ✅ Factor (3 levels): Former or never drinker / Occasional drinker / Regular drinker
Smoking status (derived) smkdsty smkdsty smoking_status ✅ Ordered factor: Never / Former / Occasional / Daily
BMI category (derived) hwtgisw hwtgisw bmi_category ✅ Ordered factor: Underweight / Normal weight / Overweight / Obese
Physical activity index (derived) pacdpai pacdpai physical_activity ✅ Ordered factor: Active / Moderately active / Inactive

6. Needs Variables

Functional limitations (activity limitations); injury status; self-perceived general health; self-perceived mental health; self-perceived health compared to prior year | Main questionnaire

Six needs variables are present. The original single-item activity limitation (rac_1) has been replaced by six granular ADL items with semantic names (adl_meals, adl_errands, adl_housework, adl_personal_care, adl_moving_indoors, adl_finances), each recoded to a Yes / No factor. Self-perceived mental health is captured by gen_02b.

Requested 2011 2014 In Study Note
Functional / activity limitations — meal preparation adl_01 adl_01 adl_meals ✅ Factor: Yes / No; semantic rename from adl_01
Functional / activity limitations — appointments / errands adl_02 adl_02 adl_errands ✅ Factor: Yes / No; semantic rename from adl_02
Functional / activity limitations — housework adl_03 adl_03 adl_housework ✅ Factor: Yes / No; semantic rename from adl_03
Functional / activity limitations — personal care adl_04 adl_04 adl_personal_care ✅ Factor: Yes / No; semantic rename from adl_04
Functional / activity limitations — moving inside home adl_05 adl_05 adl_moving_indoors ✅ Factor: Yes / No; semantic rename from adl_05
Functional / activity limitations — personal finances adl_06 adl_06 adl_finances ✅ Factor: Yes / No; semantic rename from adl_06
Injury status (past 12 months) inj_01 inj_01 injured_past_12m ✅ Factor: Yes / No
Self-perceived general health gen_01 gen_01 health_perceived ✅ Ordered factor: Excellent → Poor
Self-perceived mental health gen_02b gen_02b mental_health_perceived ✅ White-list entry gen_02a resolved by alias map to gen_02b; ordered factor: Excellent → Poor
Self-perceived health compared to 1 year ago gen_02 gen_02 health_vs_prior_year ✅ Ordered factor: Much better → Much worse; GEN_02 = health vs. prior year (not mental health)

7. Survey Design Variables

These variables are retained in the analytic dataset to support complex sample survey analysis and reproducibility checks.

Variable Source In Study Note
Per-cycle sampling weight wtsa_m / wts_m wts_m ✅ Full-sample weight for single-cycle analysis
Pooled survey weight derived wts_m_pooled ✅ Ellis-computed: wts_m / 2 following Statistics Canada pooling guidelines
Survey cycle derived cchs_cycle_f ✅ Factor: “CCHS 2010” / “CCHS 2014”
Complete CCC flag derived flag_complete_ccc ✅ Logical: TRUE if all 17 cc_* variables are non-missing
Complete predictors flag derived flag_complete_predictors ✅ Logical: TRUE if all predictor variables are non-missing
Analytic complete flag derived flag_analytic_complete ✅ Logical: TRUE if complete CCC and complete predictors

Coverage Summary

Category §2.2 Requested Available in PUMF In Study Absent / all-NA
Outcomes (primary + sensitivity) 2 2 2 0
LOP components 8 8 8 0
Chronic conditions 19 17 17 2 (RDC-only)
Predisposing 12 11 11 1 (dhhdfc12p) + 1 all-NA (student_status)
Facilitating 12 12 12 0
Needs 10 10 10 0
Survey design 6 6 6 0

Total: 69 columns in cchs_analytic. Two chronic condition variables (ccc_300, ccc_185) and one predisposing variable (dhhdfc12p) require RDC access. Student status (student_status) is all-NA due to PUMF suppression of sdcdgstud.

 
Absent from Chronic | Marc-Andre Blanchette, Oleksandr Koval, Andriy Koval | 2026
